integrationsCards.title: Sync

featuresCarousel.label: Calendar
featuresCarousel.title: Visual calendar
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Your team’s main view
featuresCarousel.description: See who does what, when, and why. Spot overloads at a glance across the full team calendar.
featureList: Assistant suggests who can take load (name + shared projects)
featuresCarousel.example: María is at 120% this week. The assistant spots who’s overloaded and suggests by name who could take work, using shared projects. Redistribution in seconds.

featuresCarousel.label: Dependencies
featuresCarousel.title: Dependencies
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Smart blocking management
featuresCarousel.description: Instantly see which tasks block your team. Strict blocking control with built-in dependency management.
featuresCarousel.example: Design blocks development. Taimbox warns you when one task blocks another so you can act in time.

featuresCarousel.label: Deadlines
featuresCarousel.title: Deadlines
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Monthly targets per project
featuresCarousel.description: Set hour goals per project and person. Compare planned vs. actual. Redistribution suggestions only among people who share projects, with configurable load limits.
featuresCarousel.example: The team’s load is uneven. The system finds who can give hours and who can take them — only among people on shared projects — and shows impact before you decide.

featuresCarousel.label: Team
featuresCarousel.title: Team management
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Everything about your team in one place
featuresCarousel.description: Schedules, absences, holidays, and capacity. Every person has a full profile.
featuresCarousel.example: Juan is on holiday next week. The system already accounts for it and won’t assign tasks in that window.

featuresCarousel.label: Time
featuresCarousel.title: Time & timer
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Real tracking per task
featuresCarousel.description: Per-task timer in planner and My Day. “Time” view shows who’s on what right now; stop timers from one place.
featureList: Stop from sidebar or Time page
featuresCarousel.example: You see María at 1h 23m on “Landing design” and Carlos at 45m on “API dev”. Stop your own timer from the Time page without switching views.

featuresCarousel.label: Weekly
featuresCarousel.title: Weekly reports
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Weekly close & redistribution assistant
featuresCarousel.description: Each week the system builds a summary with metrics. Fast redistribution assistant: who has free hours so you can rebalance work in seconds.
featuresCarousel.example: Carlos didn’t complete 15h this week. The system shows who has free hours (Laura 10h, Pedro 5h) so you can rebalance in seconds.

featuresCarousel.label: Alerts
featuresCarousel.title: Indicators & reminders
featuresCarousel.subtitle: No more surprises
featuresCarousel.description: Visual overload indicators, budget deviation alerts, and month-end reminders — all on screen so you act in time.
featuresCarousel.example: You see the project at 60% and the month moving. Indicators nudge you to act before close.

featuresCarousel.label: Metrics
featuresCarousel.title: Metrics & profitability
featuresCarousel.subtitle: Data-backed decisions
featuresCarousel.description: Operational tracking, profitability (accrued revenue, pacing per project, operational/dynamic cost, budget by deadline), and team capacity. API integration.
featuresCarousel.example: Mid-month you see accrued revenue and pacing: if a project is ahead of time elapsed, red alert. If 100% of hours are used, full fee.

faqItems.question: How does the platform work?
faqItems.answer: You can visualize and manage your team’s workload in real time. Plan tasks, set deadlines, manage dependencies, and get automatic alerts when we detect overloads or issues — all in a visual, intuitive interface with no extra software to install.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything?
faqItems.answer: No — it’s fully web-based. You only need a modern browser and internet. It works on desktop, tablet, or mobile, with no installs or complex setup.

faqItems.question: Is my data secure?
faqItems.answer: Yes. We use strong encryption in transit and at rest, comply with GDPR and international security standards, and you stay in control — export or delete your data anytime.

faqItems.question: Can I integrate with other tools?
faqItems.answerBefore: Yes. We offer a full REST API so you can connect Taimbox to your existing stack. The
faqItems.answerAfter: includes step-by-step tutorials, token auth, table reference with sample responses, a JavaScript SDK, and quick search (Ctrl+K). You can also export data in several formats for spreadsheets or other systems.

faqItems.question: How long does implementation take?
faqItems.answer: You can use it from day one. Add your team and projects immediately. The learning curve is small thanks to the intuitive UI. Most teams are fully up and running in under a week.

faqItems.question: Is there technical support?
faqItems.answer: Yes — full technical support. Reach us by email and we’ll help with any question or issue. We also provide complete docs, tutorials, and an active community to share experiences.

faqItems.question: Where is my data stored?
faqItems.answer: Data lives on secure servers in certified data centers. We use enterprise-grade cloud infrastructure with high availability, automatic backups, and physical protection. Everything is encrypted with multiple security layers.

faqItems.question: Who can access my data?
faqItems.answer: Only you and teammates you grant specific permissions. Granular roles control exactly what each user can see and do. Our team accesses data only when strictly necessary for support, and only with your prior consent. Access is logged for audit.

faqItems.question: How are passwords protected?
faqItems.answer: Passwords are stored with secure hashing (bcrypt) so the original can’t be recovered. We never store plain-text passwords. All connections use TLS/SSL. Use strong, unique passwords and rotate them periodically.

faqItems.question: What if I want to delete all my data?
faqItems.answer: You can delete everything anytime from your account settings. Deletion is complete and irreversible: projects, assignments, history, and more. You can export your data first if you wish. This follows GDPR “right to be forgotten.”

articleWhy.seoTitle: Why Taimbox? Methodology for teams | Taimbox
articleWhy.seoDescription: See how Taimbox can improve your company’s efficiency. Learn time blocking to maximize focus and team results.
articleWhy.jsonHeadline: Taimbox: Why your task list is killing agency profitability
articleWhy.jsonArticleDesc: See how Taimbox can improve your company’s efficiency. Learn time blocking to maximize focus and team results.
articleWhy.jsonSoftwareDesc: See how Taimbox can improve your company’s efficiency. Learn time blocking to maximize focus and team results.
articleWhy.heroTitle: Taimbox: Why your task list is killing agency profitability
articleWhy.heroP1: If you lead a marketing, development, or consulting agency, you know this story: you sell a project for €5,000 based on an estimated 50 hours. The team starts working. Meetings, surprises, “minor changes,” and revisions pop up. A month later, you look at the numbers: you’ve spent 90 hours. You’ve lost money.
articleWhy.heroP2: The problem isn’t your team. The problem isn’t the client. The problem is that you’re managing tasks when you should be managing time boxes linked to money.
articleWhy.heroHighlight: This is where timeboxing comes in: a methodology that has evolved from a personal productivity trick into the operational standard for the world’s most profitable service companies.
articleWhy.whatIsP1: Timeboxing is not a to-do list. In a to-do list, you work on something until it’s finished. In timeboxing, you decide how much time you’ll dedicate to a task before you start it.
articleWhy.whatIsP2: The premise is simple yet revolutionary: <0>time is a fixed constraint, scope is variable.</0>

principle1.title: Parkinson’s Law
principle1.desc: “Work expands to fill the time available for its completion.” If you give yourself a week to write copy, it will take a week. If you give yourself 2 hours (a “time box”), you’ll get it done in 2 hours.

principle2.title: Cognitive load
principle2.desc: Deciding “what to do next” drains mental energy. With timeboxing, that decision is already made during planning. You just execute.

principle3.title: Brutal realism
principle3.desc: A to-do list is a wish list. A calendar with time blocks is the physical reality of your day.

articleWhy.agenciesTitle: Why traditional timeboxing fails for agencies
articleWhy.agenciesP1: Tools like Google Calendar or Notion are great for individuals, but they are financially blind for a company.
articleWhy.agenciesP2: When you apply timeboxing in an agency, you face the <0>“triple threat”</0>:
articleWhy.threat1Desc: If you plan 40-hour weeks and there’s a public holiday, your spreadsheet breaks.
articleWhy.threat2Desc: What if an employee is on leave while a team event is happening? Most tools subtract those hours twice, skewing real capacity.
articleWhy.threat3Desc: Your calendar says “Design Home (4h),” but it doesn’t tell you those 4 hours cost €200 in payroll while the client only pays €150.
articleWhy.agenciesHighlight: Agencies need more than a calendar. They need a <0>time-based financial operating system</0>.
articleWhy.solutionP1: We’ve built a platform that doesn’t just manage time—it understands your agency’s economy. It’s not just a calendar; it’s a brain that calculates profitability in real time.
articleWhy.solutionP2: Here is how our technology solves what others ignore:
articleWhy.feature1P: Forget spreadsheets. Our backend algorithm automatically processes:
articleWhy.feature1L1Desc: If a work week starts in January and ends in February, the system splits costs proportionally so your month-end close is accurate down to the cent.
articleWhy.feature1L2Desc: We use a Max(Absence, Event) logic to calculate availability. If it’s a holiday and someone is sick, the system knows that double hours aren’t lost.
articleWhy.feature1Demo: Planner preview with sample data. Explore hour distribution by employee and project.
articleWhy.feature2P: While traditional tools show static reports from last month, our Team Pulse uses WebSockets to show your agency’s status right now.
articleWhy.feature3P: We integrate your clients’ Meta Ads and Google Ads data via API. The result: a dashboard where you control ad spend for all your clients in one place. Spot overruns before they happen and prevent the agency from paying for human budgeting errors out of pocket.
articleWhy.scaleP: We aren’t a “low-code” tool built with patches. We are software engineering infrastructure designed for companies that take their data seriously.
articleWhy.apiDesc: Have your own ERP or CRM? Connect our RESTful API in minutes. We deliver already processed data (hours_computed), saving you hundreds of hours of mathematical engineering. <1>Full documentation →</1>
articleWhy.securityDesc: Your data is isolated at the database level using Row Level Security. It is mathematically impossible for another agency to access your information.
articleWhy.tokensDesc: Our own authentication system with HMAC SHA-256 signatures.
articleWhy.apiPreview1: Get profitability per project (Step 2 of the reports tutorial)
articleWhy.apiPreview2: API query example (allocations + hours_computed). Base URL and tokens: <1>see documentation</1>.
articleWhy.apiPreviewLegend: <0>ANON_KEY:</0> your Supabase instance anonymous key. <2>YOUR_API_TOKEN:</2> JWT generated in Settings → API & Integrations.
articleWhy.apiFullTutorialLink: See full report and profitability tutorial →
articleWhy.finalP: Timeboxing is the methodology. Our platform is the tool. You have two options:
articleWhy.finalL1: Keep managing your agency with intuition and fragile spreadsheets.
articleWhy.finalL2: Implement a system that automatically aligns your operations with your finances.
articleWhy.finalOutro: The technology is here. The API is ready. Your team will thank you.
articleWhy.finalNotice: No credit card required. Set up in 2 minutes.

guide.seoDescriptionIndex: Every Taimbox feature explained in depth: planner, reports, team, time tracking, and more.
guide.layoutDescriptionIndex: Every feature explained in detail. Pick a section to read the documentation.

planificador.title: Planner
planificador.short: Team visual calendar and task assignment

mi-espacio.title: My space
mi-espacio.short: Your personal dashboard: workload, dependencies, and planning control

deadlines.title: Deadlines
deadlines.short: Monthly targets per project and employee

weekly-forecast.title: Weekly Forecast
weekly-forecast.short: Weekly close and smart hour redistribution

tiempos.title: Time
tiempos.short: Per-task timer and live team view

clientes-proyectos.title: Clients & projects
clientes-proyectos.short: Client and project catalog with contracted hours

tiempos.introBody: The Time module lets you log the real hours worked on each task with a built-in timer. You can see what every person is working on right now from the Time page (Team menu) and stop your own timer from the sidebar or that page. Your daily total is always visible.
tiempos.introExample: María has 1h 23m on “Landing design” (Acme). From the Time page you see the whole team; if it’s your timer that’s running, you can stop it with one click without going back to the planner.
tiempos.wherePlannerDescription: Per-task timer in weekly and monthly views. You only see and control your own time.
tiempos.whereMyDayDescription: In the personal dashboard, each task in your day can have its own timer to log real hours.
tiempos.whereSidebarDescription: When you have a running timer, the block shows task, client, elapsed time, and a Stop button. It also shows your total for the day.
tiempos.whereTimesPageDescription: In Team → Time you see what each person is doing: task, client, and elapsed time. You can stop your own timer from there.
tiempos.whereTip: The Time page and timer only appear if your agency has the “Task timer” module enabled in Settings.
tiempos.howStartDescription: In the planner or My Day, press Play on the task you want to track. There can only be one active timer per person.
tiempos.howStopDescription: Press Stop on the same task, in the sidebar, or on the Time page. Hours are saved down to the second in time_entries.
tiempos.howChangeDescription: If you start the timer on another task, the previous one closes automatically and its hours are logged before starting the new one.
tiempos.howTotalDescription: The sidebar and each timer show the total hours logged today for that task (base + current running session, if any).

intro.title: What the Planner is
intro.bodyAfter: in Taimbox. It shows which tasks each person has assigned in each week of the month, with color coding per project and real-time overload alerts.
intro.example: María is at 120% this week. At a glance you see she has 3 overlapping projects and can redistribute before it’s too late.

weekly.title: Weekly view
weekly.description: One column per employee with planned hours. Ideal to see workload at a glance.

monthly.title: Monthly view
monthly.description: Cards per week with a list of completed and pending tasks. Perfect for a global view of the month.

mobile.title: Mobile view
mobile.description: Cards per employee and week selector. Designed for touch with minimum 44px tap areas.

projectPanel.title: Project panel
projectPanel.description: When you click on a project you see total budget, assigned team, progress bar, and alerts.

addTask.title: How to add a task
addTask.step1Description: In the week header or in the “Add” button in the weekly view.
addTask.step2Description: Search among your active projects. You’ll see available hours and the percentage used in each one.
addTask.step3Description: Enter the task name, estimated hours, and which week it goes in.
addTask.step4Description: If the task depends on another, select it. The system will warn you if the dependency isn’t ready.
addTask.step5Description: The task appears in the calendar immediately and load bars update in real time.

indicators.title: Load indicators
indicators.tip: The progress bar updates in real time when you add, complete, or move tasks. If someone is in red, you can click their cell to open the task panel and redistribute.

dependencies.title: Dependencies between tasks
dependencies.bodyBefore: You can mark that a task
dependencies.bodyAfter: (for example, Design blocking Development). The system shows:
dependencies.whoBlocksYouDescription: A visible badge on the task with your teammate’s name and whether their task is already completed.
dependencies.whomYouBlockDescription: If your task blocks others, you’ll see it with an icon showing teammates waiting.
dependencies.warning: If a dependency isn’t completed, the dependent task is highlighted in amber so you don’t forget it.

informes.introBody: Reports are grouped under Tracking: Operational tracking, Profitability, and Team capacity. They let you consult historical data and export via API.
informes.operationalDesc: Planning vs deadline coherence per project, filters by project and employee, month navigation, and alert projects that explain why you’re at risk.
informes.profitDesc: Accrued revenue in the current month, pacing, operating or dynamic cost, effective budget per deadline, and margin per project/employee. Month navigation with Current month/Closed month badges.
informes.capacityDesc: Weekly heatmap, average occupancy, and tasks blocked by dependencies.
informes.exportDesc: Historical data and integration via REST API for other formats.
informes.filtersDesc: In Coherence: by project and employee (respecting the department view from the sidebar). In the rest of views: by period and context.

equipo.introBody: In Team you manage the agency’s members: creation, editing, schedules, absences, and capacity. You can also see Team Capacity and Team Pulse if your agency has those options enabled.
equipo.listDesc: Name, role, avatar, and contact details. Search and filter quickly.
equipo.schedulesDesc: Set working hours per day (Monday to Sunday) to calculate real capacity.
equipo.absencesDesc: Start and end dates. The planner automatically deducts capacity.
equipo.capacityDesc: Monthly and weekly capacity derived from schedules and registered absences.
equipo.createStep1Desc: Fill in name, email, role, and working schedule.
equipo.createStep2Desc: The access user is created automatically so they can log into the platform.
equipo.createStep3Desc: Permissions are inherited from the role assigned (administrator, manager, employee, etc.).
equipo.tip: If an employee has holidays next week, the system already accounts for it and won’t allow assigning tasks in that period.

introBody.afterHighlight: . Here you see your workload, your projects, planning control, and dependencies for the day or the week.

miEspacio.introExample: It’s Monday morning. You open My space and see you have 3 tasks blocking teammates. You know exactly where to start.

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.description: Tasks that block you or that you block for others. Insights so you know what to tackle first.

projects.title: My projects
projects.description: View of your week with tasks grouped by project. Quickly see what’s pending in each one.

planning.title: Planning control
planning.description: Alerts when what’s planned doesn’t match the deadline. Tells you whether you’re short or over on hours.

teammates.title: Teammates
teammates.description: Collaboration view and team load. See who is overloaded and who has availability.

metrics.title: My metrics
metrics.description: Monthly balance (estimated vs actual hours) and reliability index for your estimates.

planningDetailBody.afterHighlight: between what your deadline says and what’s actually planned/computed. Each alert shows:

planningDetailSteps.projectDescription: The project name and the difference in hours (deficit or surplus).
planningDetailSteps.yourDataDescription: Assigned deadline, planned hours, computed hours, and balance. If you still have hours to plan, you’ll see a red warning.
planningDetailSteps.teamDescription: When expanded you see teammates who also work on that project with their own balance.

miEspacio.planningDetailWarning: If you see a deficit (red), it means you haven’t yet planned all the hours the deadline assigns you on that project. Add tasks from the planner to cover the gap.

quickActions.internalDescription: Meetings, training, and other non-billable work. They’re created as internal management tasks.
quickActions.addTasksDescription: Fast access to the same add-task flow from the planner, without leaving your dashboard.

miEspacio.quickActionsTip: You can open any week cell to see the full task panel with options to edit, complete, and transfer.

weeklyForecast.introBody: Weekly Forecast (Tracking menu) is the team-level forecast and close: per-person summary, adjustments, and redistribution. The Weekly button in My space, on the other hand, is your personal review of tasks from already closed weeks: complete, move to another week (even several months ahead), split or log hours already done and plan the remaining ones in the week you choose.
weeklyForecast.introExample: Carlos didn’t complete 15h this week. The system detects that Laura has 10h free and Pedro 5h, and suggests redistributing the work automatically.
weeklyForecast.accessBody: Weekly Forecast lives in the sidebar under Tracking (next to Operational tracking, Profitability, and Team capacity). It only appears if you have the team capacity permission.

howSteps.title: Review the week
howSteps.description: You see a summary per employee and per project with estimated, planned, and computed hours.

howSteps.title: Adjust hours
howSteps.description: If needed, you correct real hours before closing. You can adjust by individual task.

howSteps.title: Redistribute pending work
howSteps.description: Uncompleted hours can be redistributed to teammates with availability, either with suggestions or fully automatically.

howSteps.title: Close the week
howSteps.description: When you confirm, completed tasks are marked and adjustments are reflected in the planner for the following week.

weeklyForecast.integrationBody: Everything that happens in Weekly is reflected on the calendar: completed tasks, adjusted hours, and redistributions show up with a “Weekly” badge in the planner.
weeklyForecast.integrationWarning: If the Weekly module isn’t enabled for your agency, this option won’t appear in the menu. An admin can turn it on from Settings.

deadlines.introBody: Deadlines define hour targets per month, per project, and per employee. They are the reference to compare what should be done against what is planned and what is actually logged.
deadlines.introExample: Your “Monthly SEO” project has 35 contracted hours. In Deadlines you allocate: 16h to Ana, 12h to Pedro, and 7h to you. The system knows exactly how much belongs to each person.
deadlines.featuresAssignHoursDesc: Distribute project hours across team members for each month.
deadlines.featuresAdjustBudgetDesc: Override the budget per project and month if the client changes the contracted hours.
deadlines.featuresFiltersDesc: Filter by project type, employee, sort order, and hide unassigned projects.
deadlines.featuresQuickEditDesc: Tap a project to edit hours per person, notes, and visibility. On mobile this opens in a Sheet.
deadlines.relationBody: What you configure in Deadlines is what the Planner and the Planning control in My space use to show whether you are on track or if you are missing or exceeding hours.
deadlines.relationWarning: If you do not define deadlines for a project, coherence alerts for that project will not appear in My space. Projects without a deadline are shown as “no limit”.
deadlines.relationTip: Set Deadlines at the beginning of each month so that alerts stay accurate throughout the period.

configuracion.introBody: Settings groups your agency configuration: roles, permissions, active modules, and integrations. In multi-agency environments you also manage each agency and its key parameters here.
configuracion.rolesFeatureRolesDesc: Each role has a set of permissions: planner access, task editing, report visibility, team management, and more.
configuracion.rolesFeaturePermsDesc: More than 18 flags so you can control exactly what each profile can do in the platform.
configuracion.rolesExample: If you change the "can_edit_tasks" permission on the "Employee" role, everyone with that role automatically loses (or gains) that capability.
configuracion.modulesIntro: You can enable or disable modules individually depending on your agency’s needs.
configuracion.modulesTip: If a module is disabled, the menu entries and routes related to it disappear for everyone in the agency.
configuracion.agencyBody: In Agency Settings you configure the agency name, time zone, and other global options. In multi-tenant setups, each agency has its own data (employees, projects, allocations) completely isolated by RLS.
configuracion.apiBody: From the sidebar, under "Settings → API & Integrations", admins can create and revoke access tokens to connect external systems (CRM, ERP, dashboards) to the agency’s data. Each token is a JWT tied to the agency and protected by RLS policies.
configuracion.apiDocsItem1: Overview (authentication, base URL, response formats, changelog).
configuracion.apiDocsItem2: Five step-by-step tutorials (getting started, syncing team, planning, reporting, absences).
configuracion.apiDocsItem3: JavaScript SDK, REST API, filtering, and Realtime subscriptions.
configuracion.apiDocsItem4: Full reference for 17 resources (tables) with sample responses.
configuracion.apiDocsItem5: Quick search (Ctrl+K) and a fixed sidebar menu to navigate docs.

contact.seoDescription: Get in touch with the Taimbox team.

pitch.seoTitle: Taimbox Presentation | ROI and Operations for Agencies
pitch.seoDescription: Discover how Taimbox transforms agency profitability through timeboxing and real-time budget control.

privacy.seoDescription: How we protect your data at Taimbox. Information on GDPR, cookies, and data security.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: At Taimbox, we take your data privacy very seriously. This document describes what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights as a user under the European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

responsible.title: 1. Data Controller
responsible.p1: The data controller for your personal data is <0>Taimbox</0> (hereinafter, "Taimbox", "we", or "the Platform").
responsible.p2: For any privacy-related inquiries, you can contact us at: <1>hello@taimbox.com</1>.

data.title: 2. Data We Collect
data.account: <0>Account Data:</0> Name, email address, and password (stored with a secure hash) necessary to create your account and access the service.
data.agency: <0>Agency Data:</0> Agency name, employees, projects, clients, assignments, hours, and operational settings that you enter into the platform to manage your workflow.
data.payment: <0>Payment Data:</0> We do not store credit card data. All payment information is processed directly by <2>Stripe</2> (PCI-DSS Level 1). We only store the Stripe customer ID and subscription status.
data.integrations: <0>Integration Data:</0> If you connect Google Ads or Meta Ads, we store read-only OAuth2 tokens (encrypted at rest) to sync campaign metrics such as impressions, clicks, cost, and conversions. We never store your passwords for these platforms. For more information specifically about Google data, see Section 3.
data.usage: <0>Usage Data:</0> Access logs, in-app navigation, and analysis cookies (subject to your consent). See the Cookies section.

google.title: 3. Google API Services Data (Limited Use)
google.intro: Taimbox uses Google Ads APIs to provide its PPC integration features. This section details specifically how we handle information received through these APIs, in compliance with the <1>Google API Services User Data Policy</1>, including Limited Use requirements.
google.s31Title: 3.1 Google data we access and store:
google.s31Item1: Advertising account structure: campaigns, ad groups, and ads (names, statuses, and identifiers).
google.s31Item2: Performance metrics: impressions, clicks, cost, conversions, and derived metrics (CTR, CPC, CPA) aggregated by campaign and period.
google.s31Item3: OAuth2 refresh tokens necessary to maintain the connection, stored with encryption at rest.
google.s32P: This data is used <0>exclusively</0> to display campaign performance reports within the Taimbox dashboard, allowing you to monitor spend and return on investment (ROI) for your clients. We do not use this data for any purpose other than providing and improving the application's features directly visible to the user.
google.s33Title: 3.3 We do not sell Google data:
google.s33P: Under no circumstances do we sell, transfer, or provide Google API data to third parties, whether for free or for financial consideration.
google.s34P: We do not share, transfer, or disclose Google data to third parties, except as strictly necessary for service provision (e.g., encrypted storage in our Supabase/AWS infrastructure within the EU) or as required by applicable law through a court or government order.
google.s35P: Data received from Google APIs will <0>never</0> be used for:
google.s35Item1: Targeted, segmented, personalized, or retargeting advertising.
google.s35Item2: Sale to data brokers or information resellers.
google.s35Item4: Training artificial intelligence or machine learning models.
google.s35Item5: Creation of independent databases or user profiles.
google.s35Item6: Any other purpose not directly related to the user-facing features within Taimbox.
google.s36P: OAuth2 tokens are stored with encryption at rest in our database. All communications with Google APIs are performed via HTTPS/TLS 1.2+. Access to this data is restricted by Row Level Security (RLS) policies, ensuring that only the owning agency can access its information.
google.s37P: Google Ads data is retained as long as the integration is active. The Agency can disconnect its Google Ads account at any time from the platform settings, which will cause immediate deletion of access tokens and synced data. Upon cancelling a Taimbox account, all Google data is deleted in cascade as part of the deletion process described in Section 8.
google.s38P: In addition to disconnecting the integration from Taimbox, you can revoke Taimbox's access to your Google data at any time by visiting your <1>Google account permissions</1> page.

purpose.title: 4. Purpose and Legal Basis for Processing
purpose.intro: We process your data for the following purposes and legal bases:
purpose.p1: <0>Service provision</0> (contract performance): Managing your account, processing payments, providing platform features, and handling support requests.
purpose.p2: <0>Security and maintenance</0> (legitimate interest): Protecting the platform against unauthorized access, detecting fraud, and maintaining service stability.
purpose.p3: <0>Product improvement</0> (legitimate interest): Aggregated and anonymized analysis of platform use to improve user experience.
purpose.p4: <0>Analytical and marketing cookies</0> (consent): Only activated if you accept non-essential cookies in the consent banner.

cookies.title: 5. Cookies
cookies.intro: We use cookies for service operation and, with your consent, for analysis and marketing. You can manage your preferences at any time:
cookies.necessary: <0>Necessary cookies:</0> Authentication, session preferences, and security. Cannot be disabled.
cookies.analytical: <0>Analytical cookies:</0> Google Analytics / GTM to understand website use. Require consent.
cookies.marketing: <0>Marketing cookies:</0> Tracking to measure campaign effectiveness. Require consent.

subprocessors.title: 6. Data Sub-processors
subprocessors.intro: To offer a secure and scalable service, we rely on the following providers (sub-processors):

transfers.title: 7. International Data Transfers
transfers.p1: We host Taimbox's main infrastructure in <0>Amazon Web Services data centers in Frankfurt (EU)</0>, ensuring your data remains within the European Union.
transfers.p2: In cases involving providers with a presence outside the European Economic Area (Stripe, Google, Meta), transfers are covered by <0>Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)</0> approved by the European Commission, adequacy decisions, or equivalent mechanisms that ensure an adequate level of protection.

retention.title: 8. Retention Periods
retention.p1: We retain your personal data as long as you maintain an active account on Taimbox and it is necessary to provide the service.
retention.p2: When requesting permanent account cancellation, we execute cascade deletion processes that permanently purge your entire agency's history (clients, projects, assignments, integrations, timers, tokens). Automated backups are deleted within a maximum period of <0>30 calendar days</0> from cancellation.
retention.p3: We may retain anonymized or aggregated data for statistical purposes indefinitely, provided it does not allow any user to be identified.

rights.title: 9. Your Rights
rights.intro: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
rights.accessDesc: Request a copy of the personal data we process about you.
rights.erasureDesc: Request the deletion of your data (right to be forgotten).
rights.portabilityDesc: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format (JSON/CSV via our API).
rights.objectionDesc: Object to processing based on legitimate interest.
rights.limitationDesc: Request the restriction of processing under certain circumstances.
rights.cta: To exercise any of these rights, send an email to <1>hello@taimbox.com</1> indicating your name, account email, and the right you wish to exercise.

contact.title: 10. Contact and Complaints
contact.p1: If you have questions about the processing of your data or wish to file a complaint, you can contact us at <1>hello@taimbox.com</1>.
contact.p2: Furthermore, you have the right to file a complaint with the competent data protection authority. In Spain, the authority is the <1>Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD)</1>.

modifications.title: 11. Modifications to this Policy
modifications.p1: We reserve the right to update this Privacy Policy to reflect changes in our practices or applicable legislation. In case of significant changes, we will notify you through the platform or by email.
modifications.p2: We recommend periodically reviewing this page. The last update date appears at the beginning of this document.

terms.seoDescription: Legal terms and conditions for using Taimbox. Rights, responsibilities, and legal framework for agencies.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: This document establishes the binding conditions that govern the use of Taimbox by agencies and professionals. By creating an account and accessing our platform, you accept these terms in their entirety.

definitions.title: 1. Definitions
definitions.intro: For the purposes of these Terms of Service:
definitions.service: <0>"Taimbox" or the "Service":</0> The operational and financial management SaaS platform for agencies accessible via app.taimbox.com.
definitions.agency: <0>"Agency" or "Client":</0> The legal entity or independent professional that contracts the Service.
definitions.user: <0>"User":</0> Any employee, manager, or administrator with access to the Agency's account.
definitions.data: <0>"Agency Data":</0> Operational information entered or generated using the Service by Users.

acceptance.title: 2. Acceptance and Account Creation
acceptance.p1: To use the Service, the Agency representative must create an account providing true, accurate, and up-to-date information. The person registering the account declares they have the <0>legal representation capacity</0> to bind the Agency to these terms.
acceptance.p2: By completing registration, the Agency accepts these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. The Agency is responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of access credentials and for all actions occurring under its Users' accounts.

billing.title: 3. Subscriptions and Payments
billing.plans: <0>Plans and Fees:</0> The Service is offered through subscription plans (e.g., Starter, Pro, Business). Features and limits (e.g., maximum number of employees) for each plan are detailed on the pricing page.
billing.process: <0>Payment Process:</0> Payments are processed via Stripe and automatically renew at the end of each billing cycle (monthly or annual) unless cancelled beforehand. If a payment fails, Taimbox will temporarily suspend access until the payment method is updated.
billing.trial: <0>Trial Period:</0> New registrations may include a 14-day free trial period. Only one trial period per Agency is permitted. At the end of the trial, if no paid plan is subscribed to, the account may be automatically downgraded to the free version (Starter Plan) with fewer features.
billing.mod: <0>Plan Modification:</0> The Agency can upgrade or downgrade its plan at any time from platform settings. Upgrades are prorated and billed immediately, while downgrades apply in the next cycle.

acceptableUse.title: 4. Acceptable Use of the Service
acceptableUse.intro: The Agency and its Users agree to use Taimbox lawfully and in accordance with these terms, specifically committing to:
acceptableUse.forbidden1: Not using the Service to process or store unlawful or abusive data.
acceptableUse.forbidden2: Not compromising the security of the Service through techniques such as hacking, malware introduction, SQL injection, or similar.
acceptableUse.forbidden3: Not subjecting the platform to abusive traffic loads or using automated scraping tools (except as explicitly authorized via our documented API).
acceptableUse.outro: Taimbox reserves the right to suspend or cancel the account of any Agency that breaches these acceptable use rules.

externalPlatforms.title: 5. Access to External Platforms (Ads)
externalPlatforms.p1: For the profitability control and PPC integration module (e.g., PPC Monitor), Taimbox will request read permissions to your Google Ads or Meta Ads accounts.
externalPlatforms.p2: <0>Read-only role:</0> Taimbox uses restrictive and limited OAuth2 tokens ('read-only scopes') and cannot and will not attempt to modify, pause, or alter the Agency's campaigns. Under no circumstances will Taimbox assume responsibility for advertising performance, spend, or the internal functioning of these third-party platforms.
externalPlatforms.p3: <0>Use of Google APIs (Limited Use):</0> Taimbox's use and transfer to any other app of information received from Google APIs will adhere to the <1>Google API Services User Data Policy</1>, including Limited Use requirements.

property.title: 6. Intellectual Property
property.software: <0>Software and Branding:</0> All source code, design, logos, interfaces, and infrastructure relating to Taimbox are our exclusive property and are protected by intellectual property laws.
property.data: <0>Agency Data:</0> The Agency is the sole owner and responsible party for all "Agency Data". Taimbox acquires no rights over it, other than the permission necessary to store and process it to provide the Service in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

privacySection.title: 7. Privacy and Data Protection
privacySection.p1: Our database applies native Row Level Security to ensure that an Agency's data is technically and cryptographically invisible to any other.
privacySection.p2: The processing of personal data linked to service provision is carried out in accordance with our <1>Privacy Policy</1>. Taimbox acts as a <0>Data Processor</0> regarding data hosted by the Agency, and the Agency acts as a Data Controller toward its clients and employees.

availability.title: 8. Availability and Maintenance (SLA)
availability.p1: We make every effort to ensure the Service is available uninterruptedly, aiming for 99.9% uptime.
availability.p2: However, we reserve the right to temporarily interrupt the service for scheduled maintenance (ideally outside European business hours) or emergencies. We do not guarantee that Service use will be free of interruptions, delays, or imperfections under any circumstances or network conditions.

liability.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: Taimbox is provided "as is" and "as available". We expressly exclude, to the extent permitted by applicable law, all warranties, whether express or implied.
liability.p2: Under no circumstances will Taimbox be liable to the Agency or its business for: loss of profits, loss of business, alteration or corruption of reported financial data, or for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages in the context of Service use.
liability.p3: In any case, if Taimbox's liability is proven, our total cumulative liability will never exceed <0>the sum of the fees paid by the Agency during the twelve (12) months preceding</0> the event giving rise to the claim.

termination.title: 10. Cancellation and Data Deletion
termination.agency: <0>By the Agency:</0> The Agency can cancel its subscription at any time from the platform via the Stripe Customer Portal. Upon cancellation, access to premium features continues until the end of the current billing period.
termination.taimbox: <0>By Taimbox:</0> We reserve the right to temporarily or permanently suspend access for repeated non-payment (exceeding 14 days), substantial violation of these Terms, or by government order.
termination.deletion: <0>Deletion:</0> Upon confirming permanent cancellation and account closure (not just paid subscription), Taimbox will apply cascade deletions to purge the Agency's entire operational history. A window of up to 30 days may remain while automated backups complete their standard rotation to disappear completely.

modificationsSection.title: 11. Modifications and Applicable Law
modificationsSection.changes: <0>Changes:</0> We may modify these Terms of Service. Any material changes will be notified via platform announcement or email, granting a right to cancel without penalty if the Agency does not agree.
modificationsSection.law: <0>Applicable Law and Jurisdiction:</0> These Terms of Service are governed by the laws of Spain. Any dispute will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the city of our company's registered office or principal offices.

s1.p: A weekly calendar shows your time blocks project by project. See monthly load, planned hours vs. capacity, and instant alerts when you’re overloaded.

s2.p: The system analyzes your tasks: deadlines, dependencies, accumulated load. You get a view ordered by what blocks progress and real urgency — not who shouted loudest.

s3.h2: Your projects, your tasks, your control
s3.p: See every project you’re on with live budget progress. Add tasks in batch, log internal work, and preview impact before saving.

tiles.desc: Log real hours from My Day

s3.timesNoteBefore: Daily total appears in the sidebar and on the

s4.h2: Weekly: close the week in 2 minutes
s4.p: Each week you confirm pending tasks: keep, move, or complete. No long forms, no noise — just fast decisions.
s4.quoteAfter: : unreviewed tasks pulse, and work can be exported straight to the agency CRM.

s5.p: From your dashboard, see who else is on your projects, their current load, and how many projects you share — so you can coordinate without another meeting.

s6.p: The reliability index measures how close actual work is to the plan. Monthly balance shows accumulated load. Coherence checks catch issues before they hit reporting.

cta.h2: Give your team the tool they deserve
cta.p: A dashboard where everyone stays autonomous, productive, and aware of the financial impact of their work. No micromanagement, no noise.
cta.sub: No credit card. Your team can be on the panel in 2 minutes.

s1.p: Each row is a person. Each column is a week. Color bars show load in real time: green, amber, or red. One glance tells you who is available and who is not.

s2.h2: Know what will happen before it happens
s2.p: Before saving an assignment you see the exact impact. The smart reassignment assistant spots who is overloaded and suggests by name who can take the work, using shared projects automatically. On Deadlines you get the same suggestions with guardrails: who can give hours, limits for the receiver, and an impact summary before you apply. Informed decisions in seconds.

s3.p: See immediately what is holding the team back. Built-in dependency management: Taimbox warns when one task blocks another so you can act in time.

s4.p: When a week straddles two months, hours and costs split automatically. Each month reflects exactly what belongs there—no manual fixes.
s4.quote: Each person’s hourly cost is applied precisely. If someone works 3 days in January and 2 in February, cost splits

s5.p: It is not just a calendar. It is your agency’s single source of truth: time, money, people, and projects—connected.
s5.p2Before: In weekly, monthly, and My Day views you can use the
s5.p2After: to log real hours; the Time (Team) page shows live who is working on what.

cta.h2: Plan with data, not gut feel
cta.p: Taimbox’s planner turns assumptions into certainty. See the impact of every decision before you make it.
cta.sub: No credit card. Your planner is ready in about 2 minutes.

hero.subtitle: Custom schedules, absences, monthly capacity, live time view, and team health metrics. Everything you need to understand and support your team in one place.

s1.h2: Every person is unique. Their schedule is too.
s1.p: Set custom schedules for each employee: full-time, compressed, part-time, or whatever you need. The system automatically calculates available hours per week and month.

s2.h2: Vacation, sick leave, and holidays under control
s2.p: Log absences by type: vacation, sick leave, personal time, special leave. The system blocks assignments on those days and recalculates team capacity.
s2.quoteBefore: If someone is on vacation next week, the planner
s2.quoteStrong: won’t let you assign them work

s3.h2: Who has bandwidth? See it instantly.
s3.p: The monthly capacity view shows each person’s available hours after absences, holidays, and existing assignments. No more accidental overload.

s4.p: Real-time metrics on team health: average load, reliability, trends, and visual overload signals. Spot issues before they become crises.

s5.pBeforeStrong: Everyone can log real hours with the per-task timer (planner or My Day). On the
s5.pAfterStrong: page (Team menu) you see what each member is doing right now: task, client, and elapsed time. Stop the timer from there or the sidebar. Hours feed reports and billing.

cta.h2: A well-run team is a productive team
cta.p: Schedules, absences, capacity, and health metrics — all in one place so you support your team while improving performance.
cta.sub: No credit card. Set up your team in about 5 minutes.

reports.seoDescription: Per-project and per-person profitability: accrued revenue, pacing, operating or dynamic cost, and deadline budgets. Net margins and EHR from real data.
reports.jsonDescription: Per-project and per-person profitability: accrued revenue, pacing, operating or dynamic cost, and deadline budgets. Net margins and EHR from real data.

mockForecast.title: Weekly forecast
mockForecast.footAfter: compliance. The system shows who has spare hours so you can redistribute work in seconds.

hero.subtitle: Every hour has a cost. Every project has a margin. Taimbox reports connect time, people, and money so you decide with real data—not guesses.

s1.h2: Are you making or losing money on each project?
s1.p: The Profitability dashboard shows revenue, cost, and margin by project and person. In the current month, accrued revenue (prorated by day) avoids mid-month illusions; if a project has already hit 100% of hours, the full fee is shown. Target hours respect Deadline adjustments (effective monthly budget).
bullets: Accrued revenue in the current month; full fee at 100% hours
Pacing: % budget used vs % of month elapsed
Operating or dynamic cost; red margin when losing money

s2.h2: Forecast: see it coming before it’s too late
s2.p: Weekly Forecast compares planned vs executed every week. Fast redistribution assistant: the system shows who has spare hours so you can rebalance in seconds. The same smart logic—only among people who share projects and with load limits—also powers Deadlines for monthly project allocation.
bullets: Redistribution assistant: see who has spare hours
Same logic in Deadlines: suggestions with constraints and impact

formulas.introBefore: Taimbox is not tax accounting; it’s
formulas.introAfter: so leadership can decide in real time. Every figure in Financial Health is based on these four rules.

f1.title: F1 — Labor cost
f1.formula: Operating: hours × standard cost/h. Dynamic: payroll proration
f1.desc: Two models: Operating (fixed cost/h by theoretical capacity) or Dynamic (monthly payroll spread across actual hours). The hour budget (target Xh / Yh) uses the Deadline value when there’s a monthly adjustment.

f2.title: F2 — Revenue (accrued or total)
f2.formula: Current month: prorate by day; if ≥100% hours → full fee
f2.desc: Closed months use the monthly fee. In the current month, accrued revenue is prorated (fee × day/days in month). If the project has met or exceeded 100% of budgeted hours, the full fee is shown.

f3.title: F3 — Net margin
f3.formula: Revenue − labor cost (− ads if applicable)
f3.desc: Margin in € and %. Shows net cash the project leaves. Internal projects (no fee) can show negative margin; they appear in red.

f4.title: F4 — Effective hourly rate (EHR)
f4.desc: The headline metric: real efficiency. E.g. €1,500 / 10 h = €150/h (strong). With 0 hours, we show “No data” to avoid divide-by-zero.

formulas.noteAfter: Fee and hours per project (effective monthly budget from Deadlines if adjusted); cost per hour per person (Operating or Dynamic model); actual/computed hours. “Current month” or “Closed month” badges for context. Losses (negative margin) in red.

export.h2: Agency financial health; API for your stack

cards.label: Financial health
cards.desc: Per-project profitability: accrued revenue, pacing, operating/dynamic cost, and margin to the cent.

cards.label: REST API
cards.desc: Direct integration: export data as CSV, JSON, or the format you need.

cta.h2: Measure what matters. Act on data.
cta.p: Taimbox reports connect time, people, and money. You know exactly what each project costs and earns.

s1.h2: All your projects, one live budget
s1.p: Each project has its client, hour budget, and a real-time progress bar. When you’re close to the limit, you know before it becomes a problem.
bullets: Full CRUD for clients and projects
Automatic alerts as you approach the limit

s2.pBefore: Set how many hours should be completed each month per project. The system compares executed vs planned in real time. When it spots uneven team load, it generates
s2.pAfter: only among people who share projects—with configurable limits so receivers aren’t overloaded and givers aren’t left below a minimum.
s2.boxAfter: who can give hours, who can take them, and how many—per project. You see the impact (resulting loads) before you apply. You decide in seconds.

Max load cap for the receiver
Minimum load for the giver (not leaving them empty)

s1.p: Connect your clients’ Google Ads accounts and monitor campaign spend in real time. Catch overspend before it happens and save your PPC team the daily manual budget checks.
bullets: Per-client budget monitor in real time
Anti-slip alerts: stay within agreed budgets
Save your PPC team ~1 hour every day

s2.quoteBefore: Both platforms are treated as a single data source.
s2.quoteStrong: You don’t need to jump between dashboards.

Forecast updates automatically with real data

cta.p: Connect Google Ads, Meta Ads, and any tool in your stack. Control client campaign budgets and keep your agency data in one place.

ppc.seoDescription: Monitor all client ad budgets in one dashboard. Google Ads and Meta Ads together with real-time overspend prevention.
ppc.jsonDescription: Monitor all client ad budgets in one dashboard. Google Ads and Meta Ads together with real-time overspend prevention.

chart.title: Spend pacing vs ideal monthly run rate
chart.footer: Quick read: because the series is cumulative, the line always rises. If the solid line sits above the dashed line, you are spending faster than the run rate needed to finish on budget.

mockForecast.sub: Cumulative pacing: spend to date vs target run rate
mockForecast.calcBody: Project month‑end spend from the current pace and compare it to the target budget. See which accounts need action in Google Ads or limit changes in Meta (campaign, ad set, or account).
mockForecast.paceBody: To land on budget, lower the average daily spend versus the current pace.

hero.titleAfter: for all your clients from one place.
hero.subtitle: Stop opening twenty Google and Meta tabs every morning. Taimbox unifies campaign spend in real time and warns you before you blow the agreed budget.
hero.ctaSub: Free Business trial · Sync in two clicks

problem.h2: One slip in Ads costs a fortune
problem.p: In Google Ads, misconfigured daily budget can explode spend. In Meta, limits split across campaign, ad set, and shared budget—the risk (overspend) is the same, but the failure mode differs. Stop living in spreadsheets and manual morning checks.
problem.quoteAfter: A spend-cap mistake (especially in Google, where “daily budget” is explicit) can burn through the client’s agreed budget several times in days.

features.title: Centralized dashboard
features.desc: See every client’s consumption in under five seconds. Instantly spot accounts at risk of overspend, trending up, down, or under control.

features.title: Forecast calculator
features.desc: The system extrapolates pacing to forecast month‑end spend and suggests a daily target to land on budget (in Meta you compare against the goal you set in Taimbox).

features.title: Profitability metrics
features.desc: Track spend and performance: global ROAS, CPA, CPC, and CTR per account—no manual cross‑tabs.

features.title: On-demand manual sync
features.desc: You choose when to refresh. Hit one button in the dashboard and pull live Google Ads API data—no scheduled lag.

advanced.p: Go beyond charts: live spend projections, corporate roll‑ups, and splitting large accounts.

segCopy.p: Same Ads ID for different business models? Use keyword rules to split campaigns into virtual sub-accounts and allocate budgets.

holdCopy.p: Group clients under a corporate umbrella for consolidated tracking. Hide paused or irrelevant accounts to keep the dashboard clean.

platforms.h2: Connects to the platforms you already use

securityStrip.text: Ad data is encrypted and isolated per agency (RLS).
OAuth2 tokens protected. Taimbox never stores credentials and cannot edit campaigns.

cta.p: Start a 14‑day trial with no card and sync ad accounts in two clicks.
cta.sub: No credit card. Set up in about 2 minutes.

featuresSection.h2: Everything you need to protect margins

security.seoDescription: How Taimbox protects agency and client data with AES-256 encryption, strict RLS isolation, and secure OAuth2 connections.
security.jsonDescription: How Taimbox protects agency and client data with AES-256 encryption, strict RLS isolation, and secure OAuth2 connections.

page.heroBadge: Zero-trust architecture you can rely on
page.heroSubtitle: You manage profitability, payroll, and ad spend for many clients. Taimbox is built on strict security by design—not as a bolt-on—so unauthorized access is rejected at every layer.
page.threePillarsH2: The three pillars of our infrastructure
page.pillar1Body: We use PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) natively. Tenant separation happens in the database engine—not in app code—so a frontend bug cannot expose one agency’s data to another.
page.pillar2Title: Encryption in transit and at rest
page.pillar2Body: All traffic uses TLS 1.3 (in transit). Database disks use AES‑256 (at rest). Production database access follows least-privilege policies and requires explicit justification for support work.
page.pillar3Body: When you connect Google or Meta Ads, we never store your passwords. OAuth2 short‑lived JWT tokens are issued by the providers. You can revoke access from Google in one click.
page.infraP: Taimbox runs on ISO 27001 and SOC 2–aligned data centres in the European Union (Frankfurt / Ireland), aligned with GDPR requirements.
infraBullets: Certified data infrastructure (ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II).
Automatic Point‑in‑Time Recovery (PITR) backups retained for 7 days.
Read replicas for high availability (99.99% uptime).
page.ppcRightP1: Every performance lead’s fear is third‑party tooling accidentally changing live campaigns. In Taimbox, that is technically impossible.
page.ppcRightP2: When you connect Google Ads or Meta under Integrations, we request read‑only reporting API scopes only. The authorization server refuses to ask for write access.
page.ppcRightP3: If someone tried to pause a campaign from our stack, Google’s API would return HTTP 403 Forbidden and reject the request.
page.appSectionH2: Internal access control in your agency
page.appSectionSubtitle: Insider risk matters as much as external threats. Taimbox gives you fine‑grained visibility across your operating org chart.
page.appCard1P: Build exact profiles—e.g. a traffic manager who sees PPC but not agency‑wide profitability. Restrictions apply at the database layer before any UI renders.
page.appCard2P: By default, a standard user sees a narrow “My week” view. They cannot access full profitability, peer payroll rates, or global agency financials.
page.complianceCard1P: Taimbox does not process, store, or transmit card data. All card processing goes through Stripe (PCI Level 1).
page.complianceCard2P: Your data is yours—no vendor lock‑in. Use our REST API to export history, profitability metrics, and time tables to CSV/JSON whenever you need.
page.faq1a: We rely on industry leaders: AWS (hosting in Frankfurt), Supabase (database and auth), Stripe (payments), and Google/Meta (read‑only via official OAuth2 APIs). Our supply chain is transparent.
page.faq2a: Core infrastructure runs on AWS with Supabase managed services, primarily in Frankfurt (eu‑central‑1), within the EU and aligned with GDPR.
page.faq3q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
page.faq3a: We support full deletion (right to erasure). On definitive cancellation, cascading processes remove client accounts, integrations, timers, payroll costs, and ad tokens. Backup copies are purged within 30 days.
page.faq4q: How do Taimbox engineers access production?
page.faq4a: Strict separation between staging and production. Engineering uses synthetic data. Production DB access is rare, non‑public, and audited. Support access to your tenant requires your explicit request.
page.ctaH2: Need more detail for legal or procurement?
page.ctaP: We can complete vendor security questionnaires and IT compliance / ISO checklists. Contact us for agreements—or audit endpoints yourself via our API.

demo.plannerSubtitle: This is a demonstration with simulated data. Explore the different sections to see how the platform works. Changes cannot be made in demo mode.
demo.weeklyLoadDesc: Breakdown of planned hours per week (example: María and Carlos)
